title: Molecular cloning of SHIP164 plasmids for expression in mammalian cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7b0j2","text":"This protocol is to help with the molecular cloning of SHIP164 and the sequences of other proteins.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"arnjk","text":"We experienced difficulty in amplifying SHIP164 from a cDNA library, therefore we opted to generate a codon","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqsi4","text":"optimized SHIP164 open reading frame synthesized. The codon optimized cDNA was cloned into pUC57 and was","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"u2s3","text":"subsequently subcloned into multiple other mammalian expression vectors.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5eqvj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9rli7","text":"The general cloning strategy is described below.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"rcba","text":"For hazard information and safety warnings, please refer to the SDS (Safety Data Sheet).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Linearize destination vector using restriction enzyme digest following manufacturers protocol (New England Biolabs).
Amplify desired insert by PCR making sure to include 15-20 bp overhang homology with destination vector.

Run both cut vector and PCR product on an agarose gel (e.g., 1% supplemented with Midori Green or Ethidium Bromide for visualization) and purify the DNA from gel using a NucleoSpin Gel and PCR Clean-up kit (Takara).
Ligate PCR insert into vector using NEB HiFi assembly mixture following manufacturers protocol ( https://www.neb.com/protocols/2014/11/26/nebuilder-hifi-dna-assembly-reaction-protocol ).
creator: Michael G Hanna
